content.text:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net10.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
    <IsTestProject>true</IsTestProject>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.8.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" Version="2.6.6" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.5.6" />
    <PackageReference Include="coverlet.collector" Version="6.0.0" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\TestProject.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>

<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net10.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Exclude the nested xUnit test project so the web app build does not compile test sources. -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Remove="TestProject.Tests/**" />
    <None Remove="TestProject.Tests/**" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>

arguments.command:
set -e
SANDBOX=/tmp/controller-verify
cd "$SANDBOX"
# Add the ASP.NET Core shared framework so the stub controller types resolve
python3 - <<'PY'
import re
p="Verify.csproj"
s=open(p).read()
if "Microsoft.AspNetCore.App" not in s:
    s=s.replace("</Project>", '  <ItemGroup>\n    <FrameworkReference Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.App" />\n  </ItemGroup>\n</Project>')
open(p,"w").write(s)
print("patched")
PY

content.thinking:
87 passed, 2 failed. The 2 failures are in the Download tests where the content type is null. The issue:

In my Download tests:
1. `Download_PassesPathToResolveFullPath` - sets `ResolvedPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "x")` (no extension). So `TryGetContentType("...x", out var ct)` returns false, ct is null. Then `ct ? ct : "application/octet-stream"` → wait, the code is `TryGetContentType(full, out var ct) ? ct : "application/octet-stream"`. If TryGetContentType returns false, we use "application/octet-stream". But the error says the contentType is null...

Wait, let me re-read the stub:
```csharp
var contentType = new Microsoft.AspNetCore.StaticFiles.FileExtensionContentTypeProvider()
    .TryGetContentType(full, out var ct) ? ct : "application/octet-stream";
return PhysicalFile(full, ct, Path.GetFileName(full));
```

BUG IN MY STUB: I compute `contentType` but then pass `ct` (the out variable, which is null when TryGetContentType returns false) instead of `contentType` to PhysicalFile! 

This is a bug in my STUB, not in my tests. The task spec says:
```csharp
var full = _service.ResolveFullPath(path);
var ct = new Microsoft.AspNetCore.StaticFiles.FileExtensionContentTypeProvider().TryGetContentType(full, out var ct) ? ct : "application/octet-stream";
return PhysicalFile(full, ct, Path.GetFileName(full));
```

Wait, the task spec itself has this naming issue: it uses `ct` for both the out variable and the result variable. That's actually invalid C# (can't have `var ct` and `out var ct` in the same scope as the conditional). Let me re-read the task:

"derive content type via `new Microsoft.AspNetCore.StaticFiles.FileExtensionContentTypeProvider().TryGetContentType(full, out var ct) ? ct : "application/octet-stream";`, then `return PhysicalFile(full, ct, Path.GetFileName(full));`"

So the task intends: compute the content type (calling it `ct`), store in `ct`, then pass `ct` to PhysicalFile. The pseudo-code is a bit loose. The CORRECT implementation would be:
```csharp
var ct = new FileExtensionContentTypeProvider().TryGetContentType(full, out var detected) ? detected : "application/octet-stream";
return PhysicalFile(full, ct, Path.GetFileName(full));
```

My stub had a bug where I named the result `contentType` but passed `ct` (the out param). That's my stub's bug, not the test's bug.

So the test `Download_PassesPathToResolveFullPath` uses a path "x" with no extension, expecting... actually it doesn't check the content type. It just checks that ResolvePath was set. But the PhysicalFile call fails because the stub passes null ct.

The test `Download_UsesOctetStream_WhenContentTypeUnknown` expects "application/octet-stream" but the stub passes null.

Both failures are due to my STUB bug (passing null). The tests themselves are CORRECT - they expect the proper behavior. Let me fix the stub and re-run to confirm all tests pass.

Let me fix the stub's Download method to use the correct variable.

Actually wait - `Download_PassesPathToResolveFullPath` sets ResolvedPath to "x" (no extension). When the controller is implemented correctly, TryGetContentType returns false for "x" (no extension), so ct = "application/octet-stream". Then PhysicalFile(full, "application/octet-stream", "x") succeeds. The test only checks `fake.ResolvePath == "some/file.bin"`. It doesn't check the content type. So with a correct implementation, this test passes.

Let me fix the stub.
